title: Quiz: The QPE Algorithm
description: Test your understanding of the QPE circuit and algorithm

questions.question: In QPE, the counting register is initialized to:
questions.explanation: Counting qubits start in |0⟩ and Hadamards create uniform superposition.

questions.question: The controlled-U^(2^k) operations create a state proportional to:
questions.explanation: Each |j⟩ picks up phase e^(2πi·j·φ) from the controlled-U operations.

questions.question: Why is the inverse QFT applied (not forward QFT)?

options.text: Inverse QFT converts phase-encoded state to computational basis

questions.explanation: The phase-encoded state Σⱼ e^(2πi·j·φ)|j⟩ is the QFT of |N·φ⟩. Inverse QFT recovers this.

questions.question: QPE with n counting qubits can estimate φ to exactly n bits of precision.
questions.explanation: Only when φ is exactly representable as k/2^n. Otherwise, there's some probability spread over nearby values.
